title: Java G1 Synchronization
description: Review the Java 26 G1 throughput change that reduces synchronization in write barriers.
learningGoals: Recognize JEP 522 as a HotSpot implementation change, not a source-code API.
Connect G1 write barriers and card-table updates to reduced synchronization overhead.

completionChecks.prompt: What is the main goal of the Java 26 G1 synchronization change?
choices: Reduce synchronization between application and GC threads to improve throughput
completionChecks.explanation: JEP 522 improves G1 internals by reducing synchronization in card-table work and write barriers. It is not a new source-level API.

completionChecks.prompt: What does -XX:+UseG1GC do in this lesson?
choices: Selects the G1 collector at JVM startup
completionChecks.explanation: -XX:+UseG1GC is a launcher flag that selects G1. The Java 26 improvement changes G1 internals behind that choice.

completionChecks.prompt: Which phrase best describes the feature's learner-facing impact?
choices: Existing G1 users may see throughput improvements without changing source code
Every application must switch away from G1
The mock exam treats it as Java SE 25 packaging content
completionChecks.explanation: The feature is an implementation improvement. It keeps user interaction stable while reducing synchronization in G1's internal write-barrier/card-table path.
